channel.title: Home on Lukas Gaete
channel.description: Recent content in Home on Lukas Gaete

item.title: How to set up your Android Pentesting environment
item.description:
As cybersecurity consultants, it’s pretty common to come across Android apps that need to be audited. In this short post, I’ll show you a quick and easy way to set up your own lab to make things smoother. I’ll also share some tips on how I usually approach Android app pentesting and what I like to focus on during an assessment.

A bit of theory never hurts, so here we go: First, it’s important to understand that Android applications come in .apk format. This format is a variation of Java’s .jar files and is used to package and distribute components for Android devices. Basically, an APK is just a compressed ZIP file with a different extension, which means it can be opened and inspected using any archive tool — something we’ll look at later on.

item.title: PWN de Pascua Writeups
item.description: Writeups of the CTF “Pwn de Pascua” challenges organized by Q4 Hacking Sessions

item.title: About
item.description:
My name is Lukas Gaete 🇨🇱, also known as abund4nt. I’m a 19-year-old Security Researcher currently working as a Penetration Tester at NIVEL4 Cybersecurity. I specialize in offensive security, with a strong focus on vulnerability research, penetration testing, and bug hunting. I’m deeply passionate about cybersecurity and continuously expanding my skills to stay ahead in this rapidly evolving field.

I’m driven by innovation and thrive on solving complex challenges with a creative and strategic mindset. I work quickly and efficiently, often uncovering solutions where others hit roadblocks. My ability to think critically, adapt fast, and deliver impactful results makes me a highly productive and valuable asset to any security team.
